expressions.english: to help someone in his/her work
expressions.notes: 일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion

expressions.english: to help each other
to give someone a hand
Please help me.
We need to help each other.
expressions.notes: -아/어/여야죠 = You should + V; We need to + V

expressions.english: Can I give you a hand?
expressions.notes:
제 = (polite) my; I
-아/어 드릴다 = (honorific) to do something for someone
-(으)ㄹ까요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.english: When can you help me?
expressions.notes:
언제 = when
-(으)ㄹ 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a chance that + S + V

expressions.english: Thank you for helping me.
Can you help me with this?
expressions.notes:
이것 = this; this thing
좀 = a little; please
-(으)ㄹ 수 있어요? = Can you + V?; Could you + V?

expressions.english: How can I help you?

expressions.korean: 도을 수 있을 때 많이 도와 드릴게요.
expressions.english: Let me help you out a lot while I can.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.
-(으)ㄹ께 = will; be going to + V

expressions.korean: 제가 도울 일 있으면 이야기해 주세요.
expressions.english: If there is something I can help you with, please let me know.

expressions.korean: 제가 도울 수 있는 일이 아닌 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I don't think that's something I can help you with. I don't think I can help with this.
expressions.notes:
아니다 = to be not
-(으)ㄴ 것 같다 = It seems/looks like + S + V; I think + S + V

expressions.english: My friend promised to help me with my homework.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
숙제 = homework
-기로 약속하다 = to promise to + V

expressions.korean: 친구가 이사하는 것 도와주러 갈 거에요.
expressions.english: I'm going to go to help my friend move.
expressions.notes:
이사하다 = to move in/out
-(으)러 가다 = to go to + V

expressions.korean: 제가 돕겠다고 했는데 그 사람이 거절 했어요.
expressions.english: I offered to help, but he refused.
expressions.notes:
-다고 하다 = indirect ending - used to refer to what someone said
그 사람 = that person; he, she
거절하다 = to reject, refuse, turn down

expressions.korean: 친구가 도와줘서 일이 생각보다 빨리 끝났어요.
expressions.english: Thanks to my friend's help, things have finished earlier than I expected.
expressions.notes:
생각보다 = " than one thought"
끝나다 = to end; to be over

expressions.korean: 지금까지 도와준 사람들에게 선물을 주고 싶어요.
expressions.english: I want to give presents to the people who have helped (me) so far.
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
-까지 = until; to
-들 = is a suffix used to indicate plural.
사람 = person, people
선물 = gift, present
-고 싶다 = to want to + V

expressions.korean: 저도 돕고 싶은데 지금 시간이 없어서 가 봐야 할 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I also want to help you, but I don't have time now, so I think I should get going.
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour
없다 = to not have, not exist; to be not there
가 봐야 하다 = have to leave
-(으)ㄹ 것 같다 = I think + S + V; will/be going to; it seems like + S + V will/be going to

expressions.korean: 힘든 일이 있으면 언제든지 이야기하세요, 제가 도와 드릴게요.
expressions.english: If you have a challenging task, tell me at any time. I'll help you.
expressions.notes:
힘들다 = to be difficult, hard, tough; to have a hard time; to be tired
힘든 is the adjective form of 힘들다.
언제든지 = whenever
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative
